concept.title: Understanding rule sets
concept.shortdesc: A rule set is a JSON file that tells markdown-validator what to check in a Markdown document. It contains two sections: rules, which defines individual checks, and workflows, which chains rules together with conditional logic.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Learn how markdown-validator rule sets are structured: the rules section, header vs. body rules, and optional workflow chains.

section.title: Rule set structure
section.p: A rule set JSON file has the following top-level structure:

dlentry.dd: An array of rules that check the YAML front-matter metadata at the top of each Markdown file. Use header rules to validate fields like ms.topic, ms.date, title, and description.
An array of rules that check the document content — headings, section structure, text patterns, paragraph length. Body rules use XPath expressions to target specific elements in the rendered HTML.

An optional array of workflow definitions. Workflows chain multiple rules together with conditional logic ("if rule A passes, then also check rule B"). If you do not need conditional logic, this array can be empty or omitted.

row.entry: Required fields exist, field values match allowed values, date is recent
Heading count, section order, required headings present, title starts with keyword

section.title: A minimal rule set example
section.p: This rule set checks two things: the ms.topic front-matter field must equal tutorial, and the document body must contain exactly one H1 heading.

ul.li: To understand each field in a rule, see Anatomy of a validation rule.
To create your first rule set file, see Create a rule set file.
